title: Mildly Spiced Egyptian Chicken Tagine
description: A citrusy and pillowy Egyptian dish featuring dark chocolate chips, perfect for a solo dinner, made with halal-friendly ingredients and finished with a flambe touch.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chicken to the pot and cook until it is browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the ground cumin, ground coriander, salt, and black pepper to the pot and stir to combine.
Pour in the orange juice and chicken broth, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
Stir in the dark chocolate chips and continue to simmer for 2 minutes, or until they are melted and the sauce is smooth.
Remove the pot from the heat and flambe the dish with a match or lighter, being careful not to burn yourself.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
